_comment: Copy to /opt/claude-stats/config.json (NEVER commit it). Per-machine settings + secrets.
_timezone: IANA name, e.g. 'America/New_York' or 'UTC'. All hour/day bucketing uses this.
_server: Short label for THIS machine, shown in the JSON and used by deploy.sh.
_project_granularity: How sessions are grouped into projects[]. 'user' (default): one project per Linux user (named after the account, or ~/projectname.txt) — the historical behaviour. 'directory': one project per Claude Code working directory, named from each transcript's cwd — use this if you run several projects under ONE user account. Override per-run with --project-granularity. Full guide: docs/projects-layout.md.
_live_monitor: OPTIONAL per-machine tuning for the working/idle activity monitor. Leave {} to use the defaults, which are hardware-INDEPENDENT (detection is network + transcript based, so the same values work across machines). Override a key only if a box misbehaves. State signals: net_bytes_per_s (remote-socket bytes/s that counts as active, default 300; idle is ~0 so this rarely needs changing) and turn_signal (default true: hold 'working' while the transcript shows the model owes a response, covering long silent API latency) capped by turn_cap_s (default 300). Diagnostic-only (measured, not in the decision): io_bytes_per_s (default 256), transcript_signal (default true). Timing: idle_debounce_s (default 8), active_debounce_s (default 2), sample_interval_s (default 2).
_alias: Your PUBLIC competitor name (e.g. 'Maverick'). This is the ONLY identity that leaves the box — never your username/email/projects. Shown on your friend's leaderboard.
_peers: Friends to pull head-to-head stats FROM. Each: {"url": "https://their-domain/competitor.json", "token": "<the PEER token THEY issued you>", "name": "optional-label"}. Leave [] until a friend is live. For a fair race both sides should run the same timezone.
